chunk:
# ===================================================================
# Netflix Data Concept
#
# Description: A comprehensive semantic model for Netflix analytics,
# designed for high-fidelity NLQ-to-SQL generation with Graph RAG.
# ===================================================================
metrics:
  - metric_name: "Invoice Total Amount"
    description: "Calculated numeric value of the billed amount, extracted from the 'total' column by removing currency symbols. Essential for aggregations."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    calculation_logic: "CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(total, '$', ''), '€', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2))"
    data_type: "DECIMAL(10,2)"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "What is the sum of all invoice totals?"
        sql_snippet: "SELECT SUM(CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(i.total, '$', ''), '€', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2))) FROM invoice i"
      - natural_language_query: "What is the average invoice amount for 'Premium' plans?"
        sql_snippet: "SELECT AVG(CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(i.total, '$', ''), '€', '') AS DECIMAL(10,2))) FROM invoice i WHERE i.plan = 'Premium'"
  - metric_name: "Service Start Date"
    description: "Derived start date of the service period, extracted from the 'service_period' string. Use for filtering by service start."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    calculation_logic: "TO_DATE(SPLIT_PART(service_period, ' to ', 1), 'YYYY-MM-DD')"
    data_type: "DATE"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "Find invoices where the service started in August 2017."
        sql_snippet: "SELECT * FROM invoice i WHERE TO_DATE(SPLIT_PART(i.service_period, ' to ', 1), 'YYYY-MM-DD') BETWEEN '2017-08-01' AND '2017-08-31'"
  - metric_name: "Service End Date"
    description: "Derived end date of the service period, extracted from the 'service_period' string. Use for filtering by service end."
    applies_to_entity: "invoice"
    calculation_logic: "TO_DATE(SPLIT_PART(service_period, ' to ', 2), 'YYYY-MM-DD')"
    data_type: "DATE"
    nlq_examples:
      - natural_language_query: "Count invoices whose service period ended in 2019."
        sql_snippet: "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM invoice i WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE(SPLIT_PART(i.service_period, ' to ', 2), 'YYYY-MM-DD')) = 2019"

relationships:
  - name: "streams_to_customer"
    semantic_description: "Links a customer's individual streaming events to their main account profile. This is used to analyze user-specific viewing habits."
    from_entity: "streams"
    to_entity: "customer"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - from_column: "customer_id"
        to_column: "customer_id"
  - name: "streams_to_content"
    semantic_description: "Connects a specific streaming event to the content that was watched. This is essential for analyzing the performance of individual titles."
    from_entity: "streams"
    to_entity: "content"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - from_column: "content_id"
        to_column: "content_id"
  - name: "invoices_to_customer"
    semantic_description: "Associates a customer's billing records with their main account profile. This is critical for all revenue and subscription analysis."
    from_entity: "invoice"
    to_entity: "customer"
    cardinality: "MANY_TO_ONE"
    join_on:
      - from_column: "customer_id"
        to_column: "customer_id"
  - name: "invoice_to_streams"
    semantic_description: "A multi-hop relationship connecting billing information to streaming activity via the customer. This enables analysis of viewing behavior based on subscription plan or payment history."
    from_entity: "invoice"
    to_entity: "streams"
    cardinality: "ONE_TO_MANY"
    join_path:
      - from: "invoice"
        to: "customer"
        on: "customer_id"
      - from: "customer"
        to: "streams"
        on: "customer_id"

entities:
  - entity_name: "streams"
    business_concept: "Streaming Event"
    domain: "Engagement"
    description: "Atomic streaming events. Primary fact table for engagement metrics, recording every instance a user watches content."
    aliases: ["viewing_history", "plays", "stream_events"]
    base_table:
      database: "Netflix"
      schema: "Consum"
      table: "streams"
    columns:
      - name: "stream_id"
        description: "Unique identifier for each individual stream event."
        data_type: "INT"
        is_primary_key: true
        sample_values: [2, 3, 5, 10]
      - name: "customer_id"
        description: "Foreign key linking to the 'customer' entity, identifying the user who initiated the stream."
        aliases: ["user_id", "subscriber_id"]
        data_type: "INT"
        is_foreign_key: true
        references_entity: "customer"
        sample_values: [48, 92, 105, 210]
      - name: "content_id"
        description: "Foreign key linking to the 'content' entity, identifying the specific title or episode streamed."
        aliases: ["title_id", "movie_id", "show_id"]
        data_type: "INT"
        is_foreign_key: true
        references_entity: "content"
        sample_values: [91, 2, 55, 134]
      - name: "stream_date"
        description: "Calendar date when the stream occurred, in 'DD-Mon-YYYY' format."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(11)"
        sample_values: ["04-Sep-2019", "25-Jan-2019", "11-May-2020"]
      - name: "stream_time"
        description: "Local start time of the stream, in 'HH:MI AM/PM' format."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(8)"
        sample_values: ["8:39 PM", "9:47 PM", "11:15 AM"]
      - name: "stream_duration"
        description: "The total viewing duration of the stream in minutes."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [22, 85, 121, 45]
      - name: "stream_rating"
        description: "User-provided rating for the content, on a scale of 1 to 5 stars. NULL indicates no rating was provided."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [3, 4, null, 5, 1]
  - entity_name: "invoice"
    business_concept: "Subscription Bill"
    domain: "Revenue"
    description: "Subscription billing records. This is the primary table for revenue analysis and tracking customer payment history."
    aliases: ["billing", "subscriptions", "payments"]
    base_table:
      database: "Netflix"
      schema:

entities.name: STREAMS
entities.description: Atomic streaming events serving as the primary fact table for engagement metrics, recording every instance of user content consumption with attributes like stream duration and user ratings.}

entities.name: INVOICE
entities.description: Subscription billing records functioning as the primary table for revenue analysis and customer payment history tracking, containing details like total amounts and service periods.}

entities.name: CUSTOMER
entities.description: Individuals with subscription accounts, associated with streaming activities and billing records through unique identifiers for behavioral and demographic analysis.}

entities.name: CONTENT
entities.description: Movies, TV shows, or media available for streaming, linked to viewing events and analyzed for performance metrics like popularity and engagement.}

entities.name: CUSTOMER_ID
entities.description: Unique identifier for customers, used across entities (streams, invoice, customer) to establish relationships and enable joins for unified analysis.}

entities.name: CONTENT_ID
entities.description: Unique identifier for content items, enabling linkage between streaming events and content details for title-specific performance tracking.}

entities.name: STREAM_ID
entities.description:
Primary key for individual streaming events, uniquely identifying each content playback instance in the streams table.}
Unique identifier for each individual stream event, serving as the primary key of the streams entity.}

entities.name: TOTAL
entities.description:
Raw invoice amount field containing currency symbols, stored in the invoice entity as the basis for metric calculations.}
Invoice amount field containing currency symbols, used as the base for deriving the 'Invoice Total Amount' metric through transformation.}

entities.name: SERVICE_PERIOD
entities.description:
Date range string (e.g., 'start to end') in invoices, parsed to extract service start/end dates for temporal filtering and analysis.}
Date range string (e.g., 'start to end') in the invoice entity, used to derive service start/end dates.}

entities.name: PLAN
entities.description:
Subscription tier designation (e.g., 'Premium') associated with invoices, stored in the invoice entity.}
Subscription tier (e.g., 'Premium') associated with invoices, used to segment revenue calculations and viewing behavior analysis.}

entities.name: INVOICE TOTAL AMOUNT
entities.description:
Calculated decimal value derived from total attribute after currency removal, essential for revenue aggregation and financial reporting.}
Derived decimal value from invoice totals after removing currency symbols, essential for revenue aggregation and plan-based comparisons.}

entities.name: SERVICE START DATE
entities.description:
Calculated date extracted from service_period strings, enabling time-based filtering of invoices (e.g., monthly cohorts).}
Derived date extracted from service_period attribute, enabling temporal filtering of invoices based on service initiation.}

entities.name: SERVICE END DATE
entities.description:
Derived date extracted from service_period attribute, used for retention analysis and subscription lifecycle tracking.}
Derived date marking the conclusion of a service period, used for retention analysis and subscription lifecycle tracking.}

entities.name: STREAM_DATE
entities.description: Calendar date when the stream occurred, in 'DD-Mon-YYYY' format, recorded in the streams entity.}

entities.name: STREAM_TIME
entities.description: Local start time of the stream, in 'HH:MI AM/PM' format, recorded in the streams entity.}

entities.name: STREAM_DURATION
entities.description: Total viewing duration in minutes for each streaming event, captured in the streams entity.}

entities.name: STREAM_RATING
entities.description: User-provided rating (1-5 stars) for streamed content, recorded in the streams entity with NULL indicating unrated items.}

profiles using customer_id for revenue attribution and subscription pattern analysis.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="TOTAL">      <data key="d4">12.0</data>      <data key="d5">Base financial attribute storing raw amounts in invoice entity, directly used in metric derivations.Base attribute storing raw invoice amounts, directly used in metric calculations after data cleansing.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="SERVICE_PERIOD">      <data key="d4">12.0</data>      <data key="d5">Raw temporal attribute containing date ranges in invoices, parsed into start/end date metrics.Raw attribute containing date ranges, parsed into start/end date metrics for temporal analytics.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="PLAN">      <data key="d4">12.0</data>      <data key="d5">Attribute categorizing subscription tiers, enabling segmentation in revenue and engagement analysis.Categorical attribute defining subscription tiers in invoices for segmentation analysis.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="INVOICE TOTAL AMOUNT">      <data key="d4">16.0</data>      <data key="d5">Core revenue metric derived from total attribute, critical for financial aggregation and reporting.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="SERVICE START DATE">      <data key="d4">16.0</data>      <data key="d5">Derived temporal metric extracted from service_period, used for cohort-based invoice filtering.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="INVOICE" target="SERVICE END DATE">      <data key="d4">2.0</data>      <data key="d5">Calculated date metric marking service conclusion, supporting churn and retention analysis.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="CUSTOMER" target="CUSTOMER_ID">      <data key="d4">18.0</data>      <data key="d5">Primary key attribute uniquely identifying each customer entity for account management and cross-entity joins.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>    <edge source="CONTENT" target="CONTENT_ID">      <data key="d4">18.0</data>      <data key="d5">Primary key attribute uniquely identifying content items for catalog management and viewership analysis.</data>      <data key="d6">64c4fd29caa1dce291f781b30eae012f</data>    </edge>  </graph></graphml>

chunk:
"
        sample_values: [22, 85, 121, 45]
      - name: "stream_rating"
        description: "User-provided rating for the content, on a scale of 1 to 5 stars. NULL indicates no rating was provided."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [3, 4, null, 5, 1]
  - entity_name: "invoice"
    business_concept: "Subscription Bill"
    domain: "Revenue"
    description: "Subscription billing records. This is the primary table for revenue analysis and tracking customer payment history."
    aliases: ["billing", "subscriptions", "payments"]
    base_table:
      database: "Netflix"
      schema: "Consum"
      table: "invoice"
    columns:
      - name: "invoice_number"
        description: "Unique identifier for each invoice record."
        data_type: "INT"
        is_primary_key: true
        sample_values: [68584, 86500, 22304]
      - name: "customer_id"
        description: "Foreign key linking to the 'customer' entity."
        data_type: "INT"
        is_foreign_key: true
        references_entity: "customer"
        sample_values: [6, 7, 20]
      - name: "invoice_date"
        description: "Date when the invoice was generated, in 'MM/DD/YY' format. Note: This is NOT the customer's original account sign-up date."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(8)"
        sample_values: ["8/5/17", "7/15/19", "5/10/05"]
      - name: "plan"
        description: "The name of the subscription tier."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(8)"
        sample_values: ["Basic", "Premium", "Standard"]
        value_mappings:
          Premium: "The top-tier plan with 4K streaming and maximum simultaneous screens."
          Standard: "The mid-tier plan with HD streaming."
          Basic: "The entry-level plan with SD streaming."
      - name: "service_period"
        description: "The active billing period covered by the invoice, formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD'. Use 'Service Start Date' and 'Service End Date' metrics for filtering."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(25)"
        sample_values: ["2017-08-05 to 2017-09-04", "2019-07-15 to 2019-08-14"]
      - name: "payment_method"
        description: "The payment instrument used for the invoice."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(15)"
        sample_values: ["Visa", "MasterCard", "Amex"]
      - name: "total"
        description: "The billed amount for the invoice, including the currency symbol. Use the 'Invoice Total Amount' metric for calculations."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(20)"
        sample_values: ["$8.99", "€15.99", "€11.99"]
  - entity_name: "customer"
    business_concept: "Customer Account"
    domain: "Customer Management"
    description: "Subscriber master data. This dimension table holds detailed information about each Netflix customer."
    aliases: ["subscribers", "users", "accounts"]
    base_table:
      database: "Netflix"
      schema: "Consum"
      table: "customer"
    columns:
      - name: "customer_id"
        description: "Unique identifier for each subscriber."
        aliases: ["subscriber_id", "user_id"]
        data_type: "INT"
        is_primary_key: true
        sample_values: [1, 2, 6, 7]
      - name: "first_name"
        description: "The given name of the customer."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(15)"
        sample_values: ["Freedman", "Nollie", "John", "Jane"]
      - name: "last_name"
        description: "The family name of the customer."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(15)"
        sample_values: ["Akroyd", "Haibel", "Doe", "Smith"]
      - name: "email_id"
        description: "Unique email address for the account."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(32)"
        sample_values: ["fakroyd0@umich.edu", "nhaibel1@mediafire.com"]
      - name: "phone_number"
        description: "Primary contact phone number."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(12)"
        sample_values: ["913-680-9878", "480-518-4776"]
      - name: "state"
        description: "The sub-national region (state/province) where the customer resides."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(19)"
        sample_values: ["Kansas", "Arizona", "California"]
      - name: "country"
        description: "The country where the customer resides."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(13)"
        sample_values: ["United States", "Canada"]
      - name: "sign_up_date"
        description: "The date when the customer account was originally created, in 'DD-Mon-YYYY' format."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(11)"
        sample_values: ["07-Aug-2010", "11-Mar-2009"]
  - entity_name: "content"
    business_concept: "Media Title"
    domain: "Content Catalog"
    description: "Video content catalog. This dimension table contains details about all available media."
    aliases: ["titles", "movies", "shows", "media"]
    base_table:
      database: "Netflix"
      schema: "Consum"
      table: "content"
    columns:
      - name: "content_id"
        description: "Unique identifier for each content item."
        data_type: "INT"
        is_primary_key: true
        sample_values: [7, 8, 9, 2]
      - name: "title"
        description: "The official display name of the content item."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(90)"
        sample_values: ["Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief", "The Blood of Fu Manchu", "Zatoichi on the Road"]
      - name: "episode"
        description: "The episode number for TV series (0 indicates a movie or standalone content)."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [2, 19, 23, 0]
      - name: "genre"
        description: "The primary classification of the content. Can be a single genre or a pipe-separated list."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(29)"
        sample_values: ["Documentary", "Action|Drama", "Adventure|Crime|Horror"]
      - name: "duration"
        description: "The total runtime of the content item in minutes."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [73, 47, 87]
      - name: "cost_per_stream"
        description: "The licensing cost incurred per view of the content, including currency symbol."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(6)"
        sample_values: ["$20.73",

entities.name: INVOICE
entities.description: Subscription billing records used for revenue analysis and tracking customer payment history. It includes details like invoice number, date, plan, and payment method.}

entities.name: CUSTOMER
entities.description:
Subscriber master data holding detailed information about Netflix customers. Includes personal details, contact information, and account creation date.}
Subscriber master data containing detailed information about each Netflix customer, such as name, contact details, and sign-up date.}

entities.name: CONTENT
entities.description:
Video content catalog with details about available media, including title, genre, duration, and licensing costs.}
Video content catalog containing metadata about available media titles. Includes identifiers, titles, genres, durations, and licensing costs.}

entities.name: INVOICE_NUMBER
entities.description:
Unique identifier for each invoice record, serving as the primary key for the invoice entity.}
Unique identifier for each invoice record serving as primary key.}

entities.name: CUSTOMER_ID
INVOICE_DATE
entities.description:
Date when the invoice was generated, formatted as 'MM/DD/YY'. Distinct from customer sign-up dates.}
Date of invoice generation in MM/DD/YY format. Distinct from customer sign-up date.}

entities.name: PLAN
entities.description:
Subscription tier name with value mappings: Premium (4K streaming), Standard (HD), Basic (SD).}
Name of the subscription tier (e.g., Basic, Premium, Standard), defining service level and pricing.}

entities.name: SERVICE_PERIOD
entities.description:
Active billing period covered by invoice in YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD format.}
Active billing period covered by the invoice, formatted as 'YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD'.}

entities.name: PAYMENT_METHOD
entities.description:
Payment instrument used for the invoice (e.g., Visa, MasterCard).}
Payment instrument used for invoice transactions. Examples: Visa, MasterCard, Amex.}

entities.name: TOTAL
entities.description:
Billed amount including currency symbol. Used for revenue calculations.}
Billed amount including currency symbol, representing the invoice's financial value.}

entities.name: FIRST_NAME
entities.description:
Given name of customer for identification and personalization.}
Given name of the customer for personalization and identification.}

entities.name: LAST_NAME
entities.description:
Family name of customer for identification and personalization.}
Family name of the customer for personalization and identification.}

entities.name: EMAIL_ID
entities.description:
Unique email address associated with customer account.}
Unique email address associated with the customer account for communication.}

entities.name: PHONE_NUMBER
entities.description:
Primary contact number for customer communication.}
Primary contact phone number for the customer.}

entities.name: STATE
entities.description:
Sub-national region where customer resides.}
Sub-national region (state/province) where the customer resides.}

entities.name: COUNTRY
entities.description:
Country of customer residence.}
Country where the customer resides.}

entities.name: SIGN_UP_DATE
entities.description:
Original account creation date in 'DD-Mon-YYYY' format.}
Original account creation date in DD-Mon-YYYY format.}

entities.name: CONTENT_ID
entities.description:
Unique identifier for content items serving as primary key.}
Unique identifier for each content item, serving as the primary key for the content entity.}

entities.name: TITLE
entities.description:
Official display name of media content.}
Official display name of the content item (e.g., movie or show title).}

entities.name: EPISODE
entities.description:
Episode number for TV series; 0 indicates movies or standalone content.}
Episode number (0 indicates movies/standalone content).}

entities.name: GENRE
entities.description:
Primary classification of content, which may be a single genre or pipe-separated list.}
Primary classification(s) of content, potentially pipe-separated.}

entities.name: DURATION
entities.description:
Total runtime of the content item in minutes.}
Total runtime of content in minutes.}

entities.name: COST_PER_STREAM
entities.description:
Licensing cost incurred per content view, including currency symbol.}
Licensing cost per view including currency symbol.}

entities.name: STREAM_RATING
entities.description: User-provided rating for content on a 1-5 star scale. NULL values indicate missing ratings.}

      - name: "genre"
        description: "The primary classification of the content. Can be a single genre or a pipe-separated list."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(29)"
        sample_values: ["Documentary", "Action|Drama", "Adventure|Crime|Horror"]
      - name: "duration"
        description: "The total runtime of the content item in minutes."
        data_type: "INT"
        sample_values: [73, 47, 87]
      - name: "cost_per_stream"
        description: "The licensing cost incurred per view of the content, including currency symbol."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(6)"
        sample_values: ["$20.73", "$5.05", "$18.72"]
      - name: "release_date"
        description: "The original release date of the content, in 'DD-Mon-YYYY' format."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(11)"
        sample_values: ["29-Oct-2005", "07-Jan-2006", "25-Sep-2019"]
      - name: "distributor"
        description: "The rights holder or distributor of the content."
        data_type: "VARCHAR(36)"
        sample_values: ["Kuphal, Sipes and Collier", "Von LLC", "Hoppe and Sons"]
